page: Amazon Web Services c6id.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with an x86_64 architecture, offering 48 vCPUs across 24 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz and includes a 54 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core.

This instance includes 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes, offering high-speed local storage directly attached to the hypervisor. It does not feature any dedicated GPUs. For network connectivity, the c6id.12xlarge delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong performance in specific workloads. The instance achieves a strong rating in Geekbench image processing with a score of 15412.0 and in PassMark CPU encryption tests with a score of 43659.0, aided by hardware-accelerated instructions such as AES and VAES. Large language model inference using a small 135M model also shows strong performance, reaching 2456.37 tokens per second for prompt processing and 306.31 tokens per second for text generation. Other benchmarks, including stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and PassMark database operations, fall within the average range.

The c6id.12xlarge offers high compute density and fast local NVMe storage, making it cost-efficient for workloads requiring high CPU performance paired with rapid local read/write capabilities. However, with only 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, it is less suited for memory-intensive databases or massive in-memory datasets. Recommended workloads include image processing, cryptographic applications, high-throughput web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c6id.12xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. It is configured with 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio, and includes 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance provides 19 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results show strong performance in image processing, cryptographic encryption, and small-scale LLM inference, while general multi-core compute and database operations perform in the average tier. The combination of high-frequency dedicated CPUs and fast local NVMe storage makes this instance highly suitable for compute-heavy workloads like image processing, cryptography, and web serving, though its low memory-to-core ratio presents a tradeoff for memory-bound applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6id.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 48 vCPUs, 96 GB DDR4 RAM, and 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong performance in encryption and image processing.

tagline: A compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-speed local NVMe storage, and strong cryptographic and image processing performance.
